content.text: Everyone walks out of the fight. The bill walks out with them.

The Street Doc owns the long bill: scar treatment and detox — the two drains every contractor pays sooner or later. The trade bleeds from Medicine: the skill stabilizes a body; the profession is the practice that treats it afterward, at rates the squad can survive.

content.title: Street Doc
content.subtitle: The long bill

entries.label: Field verb
entries.value: stabilizing or treating on mission (+1 PP, max 1/mission)

content.desc: PP economy, tiers, and the One Name rule live on Professions Overview.

content.text: Perks — Sideline
Black Bag (entry, 4 PP): opens the trade — the practice travels with you.
Perks — Established
House Calls (T2, 4 PP): minor and moderate scars need no facility under your hands (scar severity and treatment live on Scars).
Sliding Scale (T2, 5 PP): squad treatment −30%. Obligation: the margin knows your door; refusing patients has consequences.
Detox Protocol (T2, 6 PP): your detox runs 30 cr per point and clears +1 Saturation per session.

content.label: DETOX, PRICED
content.text: Under a Street Doc with Detox Protocol, detox costs 30 cr per point of Saturation cleared, and each session clears one point more than standard care. Saturation itself lives on Charge & Saturation; the open-market provider ladder is priced on the GM's economy pages.
Perks — Name
The Quiet Ward (T3, 7 PP): once per arc, treat a severe scar at Established facilities — the funnel bends for your hands.
Pharmacopoeia (T3, 6 PP): craft antidotes and stims at material cost (see The Material Cost Law); your stims add no Charge.
Doctor's Name (T3, 8 PP): one retainer client per arc — income and a hook. Corp medical boards have opened a file.
The Street Doc's lane
The healing lanes split four ways, and the Street Doc owns what's left when the shooting stops:
Flesh in combat and shell in combat belong to the Systems tree (medpacks, Shell Splint — see Systems).
Conditions belong to the Sin Eater (see Sin Eater).
The long bill — scars and Saturation — belongs to the Street Doc and the Medicine skill (see Scars and Charge & Saturation).
Your practitioner does not appear in the provider registry. Outcomes at unregistered facilities are excluded from actuarial coverage. Please keep your next of kin information current. Be well.
off the registry means off the statistics. the statistics were never rooting for you anyway.
